abstract: The global Review of The Resource Management Program Held in November, 1989, differed from previous reviews in several significant respects. Little time was spent reviewing progress since the last reviews because we wished to focus on the future. As part of its input to the institute’s strategic planning exercise, the program set out to take a broad view of its responsibilities throughout the semi-arid tropics and to start planning the pattern of experimentation and survey needed over the next decade to identify and reduce constraints to production. within this pattern, we identified four themes which were reviewed and discussed in consecutive session characterization and evaluation of resources measurement and management of constraints improvement of production systems and the assessment of technology, markets and institutions
institution: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics
event_title: Global Inhouse Review - Resource Management Program (ICRISAT Center and ISC)
corp_creators: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics
publisher: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics
keywords: Resource management; Agricultural research; Conferences, RP5912
title: Resource Management Program: In-House Review (1989)
